title: Sweet Potato Lasagna
subtitle: with Tofu Ricotta and Marinated Bean Salad

steps.title: PREPARE THE VEGETABLES
steps.text: Peel the sweet potato and slice into thin half-moons, about ¼ inch thick. Peel and dice the shallot. Mince 2 cloves garlic. Finely chop the oregano leaves. Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.

steps.title: START YOUR SAUCE
steps.text: Place a medium oven-safe skillet over medium heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the diced shallot and a pinch of salt. Cook until the shallot is translucent, 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the crushed tomatoes, half of the chopped oregano, and a pinch of salt. Reduce heat to medium-low and bring sauce to a simmer.

steps.title: SIMMER THE SWEET POTATOES
steps.text: Add the sliced sweet potatoes to the sauce, cover with lid, and cook until tender (you should be able to pierce the potatoes easily with a fork), about 10 to 12 minutes. Set your oven to broil on high.

steps.title: MAKE THE TOFU RICOTTA
steps.text: Zest and halve the lemon. Drain the Wildwood® tofu and add it to a food processor along with ½ tsp chopped oregano, juice from half the lemon, 2 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp pepper. Blend tofu ricotta until smooth. With a large spoon or spatula, evenly spread the tofu ricotta over the sweet potatoes and sauce. Broil lasagna until the top begins to brown, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: PREPARE THE BEAN SALAD
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Finely mince the remaining clove of garlic. Add garlic, cannellini beans, ½ tsp lemon zest, as many of the red chile flakes as you'd like, and ¼ tsp salt to the skillet. Cook until just warm, about 2 to 3 minutes. Squeeze juice from the remaining lemon half into the beans and add the baby kale. Toss well to combine.

steps.title: SERVE AND EAT UP
steps.text: Cut the sweet potato lasagna into pieces. Gently remove the pieces with a large spatula and place on large plates. Sprinkle with the remaining chopped oregano and serve with the marinated bean salad. Enjoy!

tips: TIP: You could use a mandoline to slice the sweet potatoes.
TIP: No oven-safe skillet? Make the sauce in a saucepan, and assemble in a baking dish.
*These items will be split between multiple steps.
Handwritten note on step 3: printed "10 to 12" minutes crossed out and replaced with "20 MIN".
